weapons_master.name: Murdle: Volume 1
weapons_master.description: The #1 Sunday Times and USA Today Bestseller. It's a great book of puzzles, and it's heavy enough to use to kill! Order your copy today today!
weapons_master.clue: a copy of a first-rate puzzle book

weapons_master.name: Murdle: Volume 2
weapons_master.description: Uncover the secret of the Violet Isles, explore the tomb of Pythagoras, and journey to the spooky nation of Drakonia in Murdle: Volume 2, which is known as "More Killer Puzzles" in the UK! Order today!
weapons_master.clue: a book that reveals the secret of the Violet Isles

weapons_master.name: Murdle: Volume 3
weapons_master.description: Visit TekTopia, study the marot, join S.P.Y., and much much more in this climactic final installment of the First Murdle Trilogy, which is known as "Even More Killer Puzzles" in the UK! Pre-order today!
weapons_master.clue: a book that reveals how Logico and Irratino met

weapons_master.name: The Big Red Book
weapons_master.description:
A collection of political quotes by Major Red, the murderous tyrant/benevolent dictator who freed Drakonia from the Iron Tsar. For example: "Revolution is one of the few things that is easier on an empty stomach."

(Read more in Murdle: Volume 2.)

weapons_master.name: a corgi stampede
weapons_master.description:
The cutest and fluffiest way to die.

(Submitted by Venus.)

weapons_master.name: kindness
weapons_master.description:
Have you ever made a cake for a diabetic? You're half-way there. Alternatively, you can simply smother them to death.

(Submitted by Mia.)

weapons_master.name: a rolled-up newspaper with a crowbar inside
weapons_master.description: It features an interesting article from the City Times: “Someone Needs to do Something About All These Murders!”

weapons_master.name: a golden bird
weapons_master.description: This flamingo statue is worth a fortune. Or is it?

weapons_master.name: a ghost detector
weapons_master.description: It's not great at detecting ghosts, but it's pretty good at electrocuting people.

weapons_master.name: a Deduction College diploma
weapons_master.description: Framed in a heavy oak frame and covered in glass, you could really do some damage. And they say a degree in logic is useless.

weapons_master.name: a deck of marot cards
weapons_master.description: You can use these murder-themed tarot cards to read your future. Draw three now!

weapons_master.name: a chainsaw
weapons_master.description: Rarely seen in whodunits (because of the mess).

weapons_master.name: a heavy codebook
weapons_master.description: Filled with keywords and ciphers, you can use it to crack codes or skulls.
weapons_master.clue: a page with a cipher on it

weapons_master.name: a human skull
weapons_master.description: "Alas, poor Yorick, I knew him. And now I'm swinging his skull at people."
weapons_master.clue: the remains of a human head

weapons_master.name: a poisoned muffin
weapons_master.description: Not only poisoned, but also rock hard. So you could use it two ways.

weapons_master.name: a pencil
weapons_master.description: For solving a murder-mystery puzzle or stabbing somebody.

weapons_master.name: a pair of leather gloves
weapons_master.description: Beware someone who wears leather gloves. They've already killed a cow: who's next?!

weapons_master.name: a pair of karate hands
weapons_master.description: If someone is sufficiently schooled in a martial art, their hands are deadly weapons.
weapons_master.clue: someone sufficiently schooled in a martial art

weapons_master.name: a hammer and sickle
weapons_master.description: When one weapon won't do, you can use these two!

weapons_master.name: a Bookie
weapons_master.description: The diminutive nickname of the most prestigious award in publishing. The fake gold is flaking off.

weapons_master.name: a spork
weapons_master.description: At one point in time, sporks were hilarious. Now, they aren't. Now, they're just deadly.

weapons_master.name: an exploding pipe
weapons_master.description: Smoking kills, especially when you use a literal pipe bomb.

weapons_master.name: a Murdle board game
weapons_master.description: Logico wonders if it's right to make a game about real murders. But it is fun, and a great value!

weapons_master.name: Murdle: Volume 1
weapons_master.description: The #1 Sunday Times and USA Today Bestseller. It's a great book of puzzles, and it's heavy enough to use to kill! Order your copy today today!
weapons_master.clue: a copy of a first-rate puzzle book

weapons_master.name: a bottle of cheap liquor
weapons_master.description: Denatured and deadly.

weapons_master.name: a fake sword
weapons_master.description: Supposedly it collapses when it's stabbed into you. Supposedly.

weapons_master.name: a trained vicious rabbit
weapons_master.description: Check your hats before you put them on.

weapons_master.name: a saw
weapons_master.description: For sawing a woman in two, or honestly, for sawing anyone in two.
divinarray: cutting something out of your life

weapons_master.name: the ace of spades
weapons_master.description: Thrown hard enough, it could slice a human throat. Even worse, it could be used for a card trick.

weapons_master.name: a brain in a jar
weapons_master.description: Philosophers argue you might be a brain in a jar. A great rebuttal is to hit them over the head with one.

weapons_master.name: a poisoned apple
weapons_master.description: If you're a teacher and you get an apple, ask yourself if the student wants you dead.
divinarray: indulging in something bad for you

weapons_master.name: an exploding beaker
weapons_master.description: Science can be dangerous. You can overturn entire paradigms or make a beaker that explodes.

weapons_master.name: a pair of safety goggles
weapons_master.description: Ironic to be killed by safety goggles, if you ask me.

weapons_master.name: a trumpet
weapons_master.description: Half of the freshmen are given a roommate who plays the trumpet. (The other half are given a trumpet.)

weapons_master.name: a board game
weapons_master.description: The rulebook is 120 pages long, and the box contains 460 different tokens.

weapons_master.name: a pair of scissors
weapons_master.description: This could be used for arts & crafts, especially if the art is murder and the craft is getting away with it.

weapons_master.name: a graduation cord
weapons_master.description: It would be an honor to be strangled by one of these.

weapons_master.name: a heavy backpack
weapons_master.description: Finally, a use for all of those logic textbooks.

weapons_master.name: a sharp pencil
weapons_master.description: Back then, they used actual lead. One stab and you'd die from lead poisoning.

weapons_master.name: a laptop
weapons_master.description: The machine you work on. It's connected to every distraction ever made.

weapons_master.name: the infamous list
weapons_master.description: A list of all the world's children, divided into naughty and nice. A clear privacy violation.

weapons_master.name: an unopened present
weapons_master.description: Is it a set of Murdle books, or a boxing glove on a spring? Only one way to find out.

weapons_master.name: that one famous reindeer
weapons_master.description: Legally, his red nose is still under copyright, so we can't say which one. But you know.

weapons_master.name: a set of ghostly chains
weapons_master.description: You forged these chains in life. Now they'll be use to take one.

rooms_master.name: the private library
rooms_master.description: A vast collection of documents the magicians keep for themselves, hiding zealously from outsiders. (As far as Logico can tell, they are mostly dirty magazines.)

rooms_master.name: the piano room
rooms_master.description: In this mysterious room is a piano that seems to play by itself-- it even takes requests! Is it a ghost, or is it uncredited low-wage labor?

rooms_master.name: the main stage
rooms_master.description: Where only the best or most well-connected magicians perform the classics, like the milk can escape.

rooms_master.name: the close-up table
rooms_master.description: Where you watch card and coin tricks. Watch your hands and your wallets! Unlucky patrons have been known to have their pockets picked “as part of a trick”
rooms_master.feature: beside a bunch of coins and cards

rooms_master.name: the parking lot
rooms_master.description: There is only valet parking and it costs a fortune.

rooms_master.name: the computer lab
rooms_master.description: Here they have the most advanced computers that money could buy in 1982.

rooms_master.name: the campus observatory
rooms_master.description: You can study the stars here, and a secret underground esoteric group uses it for astrology.
rooms_master.feature: in a place to study the stars

rooms_master.name: the statue to reason
rooms_master.description: It's really hard to make an abstract idea concrete, so they used marble.

rooms_master.name: the radio station
rooms_master.description: It's most popular program is a series of numbers, broadcast in a pattern it's up to you to figure out.

rooms_master.name: the freshman dormitory
rooms_master.description: Where the youngest students learn the basics of rhetoric by arguing with their roommates.

rooms_master.name: the quad
rooms_master.description: The students love to hang out by the fountain when they cut class and it's nice out.

rooms_master.name: the Greek theater
rooms_master.description: A perfect theater for Socratic dialogues, outdoor murder theater, or just hanging out.

rooms_master.name: Parking Lot A
rooms_master.description: This parking lot is incredibly expensive, and within walking distance of Old Main.
rooms_master.feature: in the parking lot in walking distance

rooms_master.name: Parking Lot B
rooms_master.description: This parking lot is only mildly expensive, and it's a forty-five minute bus ride to campus.
rooms_master.feature: in the mildly expensive parking lot

rooms_master.name: the bad cafeteria
rooms_master.description: There are two cafeterias on campus, and everyone agrees this is the bad one.

rooms_master.name: the good cafeteria
rooms_master.description: There are two cafeterias on campus, and everybody knows this is the good one.

rooms_master.name: the library
rooms_master.description: They have more than one million books, including a book on every number from one to a million.

rooms_master.name: the history building
rooms_master.description: The building itself has so much history that you spend most of the history classes studying it.

rooms_master.name: the mathematics department
rooms_master.description: They are currently doing experimental research into a new biggest number.

rooms_master.name: the student union
rooms_master.description: This is where the student government sits. But anybody can sit here: there are a lot of chairs.

rooms_master.name: the big cement cube
rooms_master.description: This is a big cement cube that was supposedly donated by an early alumnus.
rooms_master.feature: beside a donation from an early alumnus

rooms_master.name: the in memoriam plaque
rooms_master.description: Unfortunately (and ironically), nobody remembers what this is about.

rooms_master.name: the chessboxing gym
rooms_master.description: Here is where the best athletes at Deduction College train to become even better.
rooms_master.feature: alongside the best athletes at Deduction College

rooms_master.name: Old Main
rooms_master.description: The first building on campus, the most important, and the least maintained. Paint is peeling off the wall!

rooms_master.name: the combination coffeeshop bookstore
rooms_master.description: The biggest moneymaker on campus. A sign offers a 2-for-$500 deal on textbooks and lattes.
rooms_master.feature: beneath a pile of pricey textbooks

rooms_master.name: the arboretum
rooms_master.description: An arboretum in the middle of campus. It's overgrown with weeds.

rooms_master.name: the reindeer stables
rooms_master.description: Here is where the reindeer spend most of the year being pampered and groomed.
rooms_master.feature: where the reindeer are usually pampered and groomed

rooms_master.name: the workshop
rooms_master.description: Is it an egalitarian cooperative, or brutal capitalist exploitation? You be the judge.

rooms_master.name: the actual North Pole
rooms_master.description: It’s marked with a literal pole and everything.

rooms_master.name: Mrs. Claus’s cottage
rooms_master.description: Here is where she bakes cookies and minds her own business.
